name: Sierra de Gádor – circular from Celín, Dalías (PR-A 113)
name_original: Sierra de Gádor – circular desde Celín, Dalías (PR-A 113)
region_intro: Hiking around Dalías offers diverse natural landscapes, primarily characterized by the Sierra de Gádor mountain range. This region features varied terrain, including challenging mountain paths and more accessible trails along natural streams like the Arroyo de Celín. The area also includes picturesque spots such as natural springs and historical water sources, providing a mix of natural beauty and cultural elements for hikers. Best hiking trails around Dalías The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Gádor – circular from Celín, Dalías (PR-A 113), a 13.7 miles (22.1 km) trail that takes 8 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain within the Sierra de Gádor. Another top favourite among local hikers is Abandoned House loop from Dalías, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This trail offers views of the valley and the sea. Local hikers also love the Berja Hiking Route loop from Berja, a 3.1 miles (4.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Hiking around Dalías is defined by the Sierra de Gádor mountain range, natural streams, and historical springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Dalías are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 380 hikers have used komoot to explore Dalías's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Dalías?
A: Dalías offers a wide range of hiking opportunities with nearly 40 routes documented on komoot. These trails cater to various fitness levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra de Gádor.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Dalías?
A: Yes, Dalías has 19 easy hiking trails. An excellent option for a relaxed walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10304052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berja Hiking Route loop from Berja&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 km long and takes about 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14596609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Porticada – Berja loop from Berja&lt;/a&gt;, covering 5.9 km.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Dalías?
A: The terrain around Dalías is diverse, primarily shaped by the Sierra de Gádor mountain range. You'll find everything from demanding mountain paths with significant elevation gain to more accessible trails winding along natural streams like the Arroyo de Celín. The region also features picturesque spots such as natural springs and historical water sources.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Dalías?
A: Yes, many routes in Dalías are circular. For a challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984192603/sierra-de-gador-circular-from-celin-dalias-pr-a-113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gádor – circular from Celín, Dalías (PR-A 113)&lt;/a&gt;. A moderate option offering views of the valley and sea is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10304073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned House loop from Dalías&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Dalías?
A: Hikers in Dalías can explore various natural and historical features. The Sierra de Gádor provides stunning mountain vistas. You might also encounter the beautiful Arroyo de Celín, natural springs like Fuente de la Mosca, and the Old Arab Swamp. For specific points of interest, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2967761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Canals of Padules&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/744872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardias Viejas Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Dalías?
A: The hiking trails in Dalías are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain and the mix of natural beauty and cultural elements found along the routes.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in the Dalías region?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de Gádor mountain range offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984192603/sierra-de-gador-circular-from-celin-dalias-pr-a-113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gádor – circular from Celín, Dalías (PR-A 113)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.1 km trail with significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Can I find trails with views of the sea or valley in Dalías?
A: Yes, some trails offer scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned House – View of the Valley and Sea loop from Dalías&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13 km route specifically known for its panoramic vistas of the valley and the coastline.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Dalías?
A: While Dalías offers hiking year-round, the spring and autumn months generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the trails. Summers can be very hot, especially on more exposed mountain paths, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during that season.

Q: Are there any towns or settlements near the hiking trails worth exploring?
A: Yes, several charming settlements are close to the hiking areas. The town of Dalías itself is a good starting point. You can also explore places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almócita&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5574390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fondón&lt;/a&gt;, which are often connected by trails and offer a glimpse into local culture and history.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Dalías well-marked?
A: The extensive network of trails in Andalusia, including those around Dalías, is generally well-signposted and standardized. This makes it easier to navigate and explore the region's natural beauty and hidden secrets.
tips: Small refuge perfect for taking a break, replenishing water in its fountain and contemplating the panoramic views.
Point from which to contemplate the hills of the southern slope of the Sierra de Gádor, in the distance you can already see some of the greenhouses in the El Ejido area, known as "Mar de Plástico".
